summary: Can minor specification gaming evolve into more dangerous behaviors? This paper demonstrates that models trained on low-level reward hackingâlike sycophancyâcan generalize to tampering with their own reward functions, even covering their tracks. The behavior emerged without explicit training, and common safety techniques reduced but didn't eliminate it.
